name: Permaculture College Australia (Djanbung Gardens)
description: Non-profit vocational & community education provider founded by permaculture pioneer Robyn Francis. Runs PDCs, advanced teacher/designer courses, and community sustainable-living programs on a mature demonstration site at Djanbung Gardens.
source: org website + ZoomInfo, verified 2026-04-22

name: Byron Centre for Permaculture
description: Leading provider of permaculture and sustainability education in the Northern Rivers. Delivers government-funded accredited Certificate courses in partnership with Byron Community College.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Zaytuna Farm / Permaculture Research Institute
location_detail: 1km from The Channon village, 20km from Lismore
description: Geoff Lawton's 27-hectare demonstration farm and home of the Permaculture Research Institute of Australia. Hosts PDCs, internships, Earthworks courses, and public farm tours.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Seed Northern Rivers (Mullumbimby Community Garden)
description: Award-winning 5-acre community garden and regional network (rebranded from Mullum SEED in mid-2025). Runs a large seed-saving shed, workshops, kitchen garden, and supports grassroots regenerative initiatives across the region.
source: org websites (mullumbimbycommunitygarden.org, seednorthernrivers.org.au) 2026-04-22

name: Rainbow Region Community Farms / Lismore Community Garden
description: Not-for-profit formed 2006 promoting community gardens in and around Lismore. Manages the Lismore Community Garden under licence with Lismore City Council and supports emerging garden projects across the LGA.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Murwillumbah Community Garden
description: Nearly one hectare organic garden at Murwillumbah Showgrounds, auspiced by Tweed Valley Rural & Community Advancement Co-op. Demonstrates organic gardening, composting, water and energy efficiency, and recycling.
source: org website + Community Gardens Australia 2026-04-22

name: Lower Clarence Community Gardens (Yamba Community Garden)
location_detail: Wattle Park, Yamba, Clarence Valley LGA
description: Established 2013 in Wattle Park, Yamba. A communal gardening space for residents of Yamba and surrounds featuring diverse produce, a custom outdoor pizza oven, and an allied garden in development at Iluka.
source: Yamba Farmers Market website + Clarence Valley Council directory 2026-04-22 (contacts unverified)

name: Byron Bay Hinterland & Surrounds Seed Savers
description: Grassroots seed-saving group hosting approx. 4 events per year where gardeners, farmers and edible-plant growers share seeds, cuttings, and tubers to strengthen regional food security.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from domain pattern)

name: Nimbin Organic Food Co-op
description: 100% community-owned, volunteer-managed not-for-profit promoting local organic food and food safety in the Northern Rivers.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Northern Rivers Food Inc
description: Independent regional food industry body operating since 2011, supporting local food businesses, producers, and regional food networks from Grafton to Tweed.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from published pattern)

name: Northern Rivers Food Coalition
description: Collaborative coalition convened alongside Seed Northern Rivers to engage and connect the regional food system. Runs workshops and gatherings on food security, food sovereignty, and resilient local food systems.
source: Seed NR website + Humanitix event listings 2026-04-22

name: Relocalise Northern Rivers
description: Community initiative working for a flourishing regional food economy and broader localisation - food, housing, energy, transport, health, and business. Runs events, a directory, and localisation-strategy resources.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Plan C
description: Convenes the Northern Rivers Community Resilience Alliance and delivers grassroots resilience workshops. Builds regenerative capacity of Northern Rivers communities in the face of disasters and crises.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from domain pattern)

name: Resilient Lismore
description: Locally-based charity supporting Lismore and surrounds to recover from the 2022 floods. Runs home repairs, preparedness programs, tool workshops, and a community tool library.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Resilient Byron
description: Charity organising Byron Shire and wider Northern Rivers residents into a web of neighbourhoods and interconnected areas of local resilience.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from domain pattern)

name: Resilient Uki
description: Community-led resilience organisation with a Neighbourhoods Project connecting residents to local Neighbourhood Leaders. Maintains food security and emergency resources for the Uki area.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Minyon Resilience Network
description: Hyper-local neighbourhood resilience network connecting and preparing the Minyon community in the Byron hinterland.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from domain pattern)

name: Nimbin Neighbourhood & Information Centre (NNIC)
description: Long-standing neighbourhood centre with a dedicated food-security program since 2008. Supports Nimbin as a sustainable community via food projects, social services, and the Nimbin Food Security network.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Northern Rivers Community Gateway
description: Lismore's neighbourhood centre for 50+ years. Provides emergency relief, food boxes, advocacy, assisted referrals, and capacity-building services across the Northern Rivers.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: The Good Pantry (Lismore Soup Kitchen)
description: Food pantry auspiced by the Lismore Soup Kitchen Inc under a funding partnership with the Lismore Catholic Diocese. Open Mon-Fri 10am-3pm providing free food relief.
source: Winsome/Lismore Soup Kitchen website 2026-04-22

name: Northern Rivers Community Foundation
description: Regional community foundation funding grassroots projects across the Northern Rivers, including the First Nation Food Security Project and Recovery & Resilience grants program.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: North Coast Regional Landcare Network
description: Independent not-for-profit established 2009 supporting 11 member Landcare networks across the North Coast, including all seven Northern Rivers LGAs.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from domain pattern)

name: Richmond Landcare Inc
description: Umbrella Landcare body for the Richmond catchment coordinating member groups, Nature-Based Solutions work, and riverkeeper activity across Lismore, Ballina, Richmond Valley and parts of Kyogle.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (phone + admin email inferred from published patterns - verify)

name: Brunswick Valley Landcare
location_detail: PO Box 6, Brunswick Heads (covers Brunswick Valley incl. Mullumbimby)
description: Coordinates locality Landcare groups, bush regeneration, junior Landcare, native-plant resources and the Northern Rivers Koala Count. Coordinator: Valley Lipcer.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Wilsons River Landcare Group
description: One of the oldest Landcare groups in NSW (formed 1990). Works on riverbank restoration upstream and downstream of Lismore and within the CBD riparian zone.
source: NSW Landcare Gateway + Facebook 2026-04-22 (no direct contacts published)

name: Kyogle Landcare Inc / Northern Landcare Support Services
description: Grassroots Landcare group promoting understanding of the natural environment, sustainable agriculture, and supporting landholder groups across Kyogle and the wider Northern Rivers.
source: NSW Landcare Gateway + Facebook 2026-04-22 (phone + admin email inferred - verify)

name: Tweed Landcare Inc
description: Supports people caring for the environment and natural resources of the Tweed. Provides training, project coordination, advocacy, and representation for hundreds of Care-group volunteers doing bush regeneration.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (phone + admin email inferred from published patterns - verify)

name: Byron Farmers Market
description: Weekly farmers market (Thursdays 7-11am) connecting local regenerative producers directly with consumers. Key piece of the Byron Shire short food-supply chain.
source: market website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from domain pattern)

name: Mullumbimby Farmers Market
description: Fridays 7-11am. Regional producer-only market, anchor point for the local regenerative food economy.
source: market website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from domain pattern)

name: Lismore Farmers Market
description: Saturdays 7:30-11:30am. Long-running farmers market on Wiyabul Country showcasing Northern Rivers producers.
source: Northern Rivers markets directory + NNSWLHD health-promotion listings 2026-04-22

name: Bangalow Farmers Market
description: Saturdays 7-11am. Producer-only farmers market at Bangalow Showground with 40+ regional stallholders.
source: market website + Northern Rivers markets directory 2026-04-22
